title: selliguea feeioides
intro: Selliguea feeioides, also known as the Fee's polypody, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Selliguea feeioides include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds, making it a valuable herb in folk medicine. It can be prepared as an herbal tea or a decoction by infusing the leaves in hot water, allowing it to be consumed for its medicinal purposes.
